title: Guggenheim Collection: Brancusi
description: Sculptures by Constantin Brancusi, all from the Guggenheim’s collection, were on view alongside photographs of the modernist master in his studio.

title: Pello Irazu: Panorama
description: Pello Irazu, who participated in the renewal of Basque and Spanish sculpture, created a conceptual device specifically for this show, which features some of the artist’s most significant masterpieces.

title: A Year with Children 2017
description: A Year with Children is an annual exhibition featuring art created by students participating in the Guggenheim’s artist-in-residence program Learning Through Art (LTA).

title: Pierre Huyghe: (Untitled) Human Mask
description: Pierre Huyghe’s (Untitled) Human Mask takes viewers to a Japanese landscape scarred by the recent tsunami and the Fukushima nuclear disaster.

title: Mark Tobey: Threading Light
description: This comprehensive exhibition of Mark Tobey’s work traces the evolution of the artist’s groundbreaking style and his contributions to abstraction and mid-century American modernism.

title: Paris, Fin de Siècle: Signac, Redon, Toulouse-Lautrec and Their Contemporaries
description: Mirroring the many facets of an anxious, unsettled era, the Parisian fin de siècle saw the emergence of a broad spectrum of new artistic movements.

title: Bill Viola: A Retrospective
description: This exhibition is a complete thematic and chronological survey of Bill Viola’s career, from his early pioneering works of the 1970s to his recent monumental installations.

title: Georg Baselitz: The Heroes
description: This exhibition is devoted to George Baselitz’s 1965–66 series of dramatic, paradoxical paintings that depict ambiguous, vulnerable “heroes.”

title: Danh Vo: Take My Breath Away
description: The subject of this survey, artist Danh Vo, uses personal narratives and varied mediums to address themes such as religion, capitalism, and artistic authorship.

title: Ken Jacobs: The Guests
description: For this 3-D film creation, Ken Jacobs used footage from an early Lumière brothers film showing guests at a wedding in late-19th-century Paris.

title: Giacometti
description: On view in this exhibition are nearly 200 sculptures, paintings, and drawings by the Swiss artist known for his distinctive figurative sculptures.

title: One Hand Clapping
description: Five artists using varied mediums, including oil on canvas and virtual-reality software, explore the ways globalization affects our understanding of the future.

title: Hilma af Klint: Paintings for the Future
description: This exhibition featured bold, colorful paintings by a long-underrecognized innovator of abstract art, Hilma af Klint (1862–1944).

title: PICASSO: On the Beach
description: The exhibition focuses on one of Peggy Guggenheim’s favorite works of art, Pablo Picasso’s On the Beach (La Baignade), and features fourteen works of beaches and bathers made by the artist in 1937.

title: Anni Albers: Touching Vision
description: Anni Albers, one of the Bauhaus’s most distinguished artists, pioneered the reinvention of fiber art.

title: Mystical Symbolism: The Salon de la Rose+Croix in Paris, 1892–1897
description: Discover art shown at six 19th-century Parisian salons, where mythical and visionary themes and images of androgynous creatures, chimeras, and incubi prevailed.

title: David Hockney: 82 Portraits and 1 Still-life
description: David Hockney returns to portraiture with an intimate series focusing on friends, family, and the Los Angeles art world.

title: Amie Siegel: Winter
description: In Siegel’s video installation, an absorbing, post-apocalyptic film joins a live soundtrack incorporating voice-over, sound effects, and music by local performers.

title: Countryside, The Future
description: An exhibition by AMO/Rem Koolhaas, Countryside, the Future presented speculations about tomorrow through insights into the countryside today.

title: Art and Space
description: A celebration of place and architecture through art, this exhibition includes important works from the collections of the Guggenheim Museum Bilbao and other Guggenheim museums.

title: A Year with Children 2018
description: This annual exhibition features works created by young students participating in the Guggenheim’s artist-in-residence program Learning Through Art.

title: But a Storm Is Blowing from Paradise: Contemporary Art of the Middle East and North Africa
description: This exhibition illuminates contemporary artistic practices in the Middle East and North Africa and the region’s diaspora.

title: Marino Marini: Visual Passions
description: Major works by sculptor Marino Marini are displayed with Etruscan objects, 15th-century Italian sculptures, and pieces by Giacomo Manzù, Henry Moore, Pablo Picasso, and Auguste Rodin.

title: Henri Michaux: The Other Side
description: Starting with Henri Michaux’s drawing and gouche works of the 1920s, the exhibition spans multiple stages of experimentation, through his ’80s canvas works.

title: Esther Ferrer: Intertwined Spaces
description: This thematic exhibition is comprised exclusively of unseen projects by Spanish performance art pioneer Esther Ferrer.

title: Michael Snow: Closed Circuit
description: In Closed Circuit, a montage of Michael Snow’s works, video is presented as an autonomous sensory channel with minimal traces of human agency.

title: Art and China after 1989: Theater of the World
description: Bracketed by the end of the Cold War and the Beijing Olympics, this exhibition surveys artistic experimentation during the rise of a newly powerful China.

title: Josef Albers in Mexico
description: This exhibition presents the paintings of Josef Albers alongside photographs and photocollages of his trips to archeological sites and monuments in Mexico.

title: 1948: The Biennale of Peggy Guggenheim
description: Select works and a partial recreation of the pavilion commemorate the 70th anniversary of the Peggy Guggenheim collection exhibit at the 24th Venice Biennale.

title: Chagall: The Breakthrough Years, 1911–1919
description: During this essential period, Marc Chagall cultivated his colorful, distinctive style, influenced by Jewish tradition, Russian art, and Parisian avant-garde movements.

title: Learning Through Art 2017-18
description: Schoolchildren worked with six artists to create the works in this exhibition.

title: Implicit Tensions: Mapplethorpe Now
description: Thirty years after Robert Mapplethorpe’s death, the Guggenheim celebrated the sustained legacy of the artist’s work with a yearlong exhibition.

title: Artistic License: Six Takes on the Guggenheim Collection
description: In the Guggenheim’s first artist-curated exhibition, six contemporary artists explored the museum’s collection, each creating a distinct thematic presentation.

title: The Hugo Boss Prize 2018: Simone Leigh, Loophole of Retreat
description: Loophole of Retreat presented a new body of work by Simone Leigh, winner of the Hugo Boss Prize 2018.

title: Joana Vasconcelos: I’m Your Mirror
description: Portuguese artist Joana Vasconcelos uses everyday materials such as tiles, medicines, and plastic cutlery as she humorously explores issues of identity.

title: Javier Téllez: Shadow Play
description: Two Javier Téllez films explore the journeys of refugees: never-ending wandering in Bourbaki Panorama and life-changing scenes in Shadow Play.

title: R. H. Quaytman: + x, Chapter 34
description: Contemporary artist R. H. Quaytman created a new group of paintings, shown in conjunction with Hilma af Klint: Paintings for the Future.

title: Van Gogh to Picasso: The Thannhauser Legacy
description: Essential works by modern masters embody the Thannhauser family’s legacy as champions of the art of their time.

title: Osvaldo Licini: Let Sheer Folly Sweep Me Away
description: This retrospective celebrates Osvaldo Licini, one of the most original and elusive personalities of the Italian art scene of the first half of the 20th century.

title: Basquiat’s “Defacement”: The Untold Story
description: This exhibit examined Jean-Michel Basquiat’s exploration of Black identity and his protest against police brutality after the death of artist Michael Stewart.

title: Alberto Giacometti: A Retrospective
description: This exhibition features more than 200 works created over four decades by Alberto Giacometti, one of the most influential artists of the 20th century.

title: Diana Thater: A Runaway World
description: Two video installations explored the lives and habitats of rhinos and elephants, and the illicit economies that threaten their survival.

title: Architecture Effects
description: This exhibition examined the state of architecture two decades after the opening of the Guggenheim Museum Bilbao and its pioneering use of digital technology.

title: A Year with Children 2019
description: This annual exhibition featured works created by young students participating in the Guggenheim’s artist-in-residence program Learning Through Art.

title: From Gesture to Form: Postwar European and American Art from the Schulhof Collection
description: This exhibit of nearly the entire Schulhof Collection offered insight into art movements and styles that evolved from the end of World War II through the 1980s.

title: Allora & Calzadilla: The Tropical Pharmacy
description: Puerto Rico–based artists Jennifer Allora and Guillermo Calzadilla investigated ecological, geopolitical, and cultural issues in this exhibition of recent works.

title: Jenny Holzer: Thing Indescribable
description: Jenny Holzer’s largest-ever exhibition featured her signature works using text in site-responsive installations.

title: The Nature of Arp
description: A founder of the Dada movement and a pioneer of abstraction, Jean Arp developed a vocabulary of curving, organic forms that influenced generations of artists.

title: A Backward Glance: Giorgio Morandi and the Old Masters
description: Casting new light on Giorgio Morandi’s work, this exhibition explored how Spanish, Bolognese, and French old masters influenced his landscapes and still lifes.

title: Lucio Fontana: On the Threshold
description: Through his paintings slashed with a knife, as well as his sculptures and drawings, Lucio Fontana became an innovative figure in postwar European art.

title: Gerhard Richter: Seascapes
description: Gerhard Richter’s Seascapes paintings draw from moody art-historical landscapes and vacation snapshots to reflect on nature and visual perception.

title: Learning Through Art 2019
description: Showcasing work by 209 schoolchildren, this exhibition culminated the annual Learning Through Art program, in which artists help teach in students’ classrooms.

title: Jesper Just: This Nameless Spectacle
description: In this two-channel video installation, two people with overlapping itineraries exchanged gazes in Paris’s Buttes-Chaumont Park and the surrounding neighborhood.

title: Peggy Guggenheim: The Last Dogaressa
description: Celebrating the Venetian life of Peggy Guggenheim, this exhibition presented major and lesser-known artworks that she collected between 1948 and 1979.

title: Thomas Struth
description: This exhibition offered a comprehensive journey through more than four decades of work by pioneering German photographer Thomas Struth.

title: Masterpieces of the Kunsthalle Bremen: From Delacroix to Beckmann
description: Featuring 19th- and 20th-century works from France and Germany, this survey explored Kunsthalle Bremen’s pioneering collection and its support of modern art.

title: Marking Time: Process in Minimal Abstraction
description: With works by Agnes Martin, Roman Opałka, and Park Seo-Bo, Marking Time: Process in Minimal Abstraction invited viewers to imagine the artists’ creative process.

title: The Fullness of Color: 1960s Painting
description: In the 1960s a group of avant-garde painters began to push abstraction in new directions. The Fullness of Color: 1960s Painting charted several of these courses.

title: Kandinsky
description: Drawn primarily from the Solomon R. Guggenheim Foundation’s rich holdings, this exhibition traced Kandinsky’s aesthetic evolution and spanned his entire oeuvre.

title: Away from the Easel: Jackson Pollock’s Mural
description: This focused exhibition was dedicated to Jackson Pollock’s Mural, a work that had not been on view in New York in more than 20 years.

title: Knotted, Torn, Scattered: Sculpture after Abstract Expressionism
description: This exhibition presented sculptural work by artists who challenge and expand the legacy of Abstract Expressionism.

title: Migrating Objects: Arts of Africa, Oceania, and the Americas in the Peggy Guggenheim Collection
description: This exhibition focused on Peggy Guggenheim’s turn in the 1950s and ’60s to collecting works by artists in Africa, Oceania, and the indigenous Americas.

title: A Year with Children 2020
description: A multimedia e-book featuring art, video, and audio tracks by students and educators participating in the Guggenheim’s Learning Through Art program.

title: The Hugo Boss Prize 2020: Deana Lawson, Centropy
description: Deana Lawson, winner of the Hugo Boss Prize, presented new images of Black diasporic identity.

title: A Year with Children 2021
description: This exhibition showcased select artworks made by students participating in the museum’s arts education program Learning Through Art during its fiftieth year.

title: Re/Projections: Video, Film, and Performance for the Rotunda
description: This series of presentations rethought the Guggenheim’s rotunda as a space for navigating tensions between collective and individual experience.

title: Off the Record
description: Featuring works by thirteen artists, Off the Record questioned and challenged dominant narratives in mainstream documentation.

title: Gillian Wearing: Wearing Masks
description: Gillian Wearing’s photographs, videos, sculptures, and paintings probe the tensions between self and society in an increasingly media-saturated world.

title: Vasily Kandinsky: Around the Circle
description: Through his paintings, watercolors, and woodcuts, Vasily Kandinsky mined the expressive and spiritual possibilities of color, line, and form.

title: Etel Adnan: Light’s New Measure
description: ​​Writer and artist Etel Adnan’s paintings, tapestries, and works on paper reflect her faith in the human spirit and the beauty of the natural world.

title: Jennie C. Jones: Dynamics
description: For her solo exhibition, artist Jennie C. Jones produced works that responded to the museum’s architecture by interweaving visual and aural experience.

title: Cecilia Vicuña: Spin Spin Triangulene
description: Pioneering Chilean artist, poet, activist, and filmmaker Cecilia Vicuña explores themes of memory, language, science, and Indigenous spirituality and knowledge.

title: A Year with Children 2022
description: This annual exhibition featured works created by students participating in the Guggenheim’s artist-in-residence program Learning Through Art.

title: Eva Hesse: Expanded Expansion
description: Pioneering artist Eva Hesse’s Expanded Expansion probes materiality, absurdity, and incongruity.

title: Alex Katz: Gathering
description: Created over eight decades, Alex Katz’s portraits, social scenes, and landscapes captured the immediacy of visual perception in paint.

title: Nick Cave: Forothermore
description: This survey exhibition honored the artist’s lifelong commitment to creating space for those who feel marginalized by dominant society and culture.

title: Sensory Poetics: Collecting Abstraction
description: This selection of collection artworks by ten contemporary artists reflected a turn towards gesture as a response to the constraint of Minimalism.

title: Gego: Measuring Infinity
description: In sculptures, drawings, prints, and textiles, Gego traced a markedly individual path through her organic forms, linear structures, and spatial investigations.

title: Sarah Sze: Timelapse
description: Sarah Sze’s site-specific interventions invited meditation on how our digitally and materially saturated world shapes the way we experience time and place.

title: Only the Young: Experimental Art in Korea, 1960s–1970s
description: This exhibition examined how Korean artists harnessed the power of contemporary art to explore pressing issues in an era of remarkable transformation.

title: Going Dark: The Contemporary Figure at the Edge of Visibility
description: This exhibition made meaning of the semi-visible figure, arguing for its significance in contemporary art as a genre of unique conceptual and formal power.

title: A Year with Children 2023
description: This annual exhibition features works created by students participating in the Guggenheim’s artist-in-residence program, Learning Through Art.

title: Young Picasso in Paris
description: Coinciding with the fiftieth anniversary of Picasso’s death, this exhibition explored the artist’s production during a formative time in the city of light.

title: Harmony and Dissonance: Orphism in Paris, 1910–1930
description: Discover the vibrant abstract art movement of the early 20th century known as Orphism in this major exhibition featuring over 90 works.

title: Jenny Holzer: Light Line
description: Jenny Holzer: Light Line presented a new manifestation of an iconic LED sign, activating the building with her pioneering use of the written word.

title: A Year with Children 2024
description: This annual exhibition featured works created by students participating in the Guggenheim’s artist-in-residence program, Learning Through Art.

title: By Way Of: Material and Motion in the Guggenheim Collection
description: This exhibition explores how artists moved beyond traditional studio practices and materials to reinvigorate art with “extramural” modes of working.

title: Rashid Johnson: A Poem for Deep Thinkers
description: Explore Rashid Johnson’s major solo exhibition featuring nearly 90 works and dynamic performances in the museum’s rotunda.

title: Collection in Focus | Beatriz Milhazes: Rigor and Beauty
description: Experience a focused exhibition of Beatriz Milhazes’s vibrant, mural-like abstract paintings that blend folklore, popular culture, nature, and spirituality.

title: Gabriele Münter: Contours of a World
description: Explore over seventy paintings and photographs that illuminate Gabriele Münter’s experimental practice and her role in shaping modern art in Europe.

title: A Year with Children 2025
description: This annual exhibition features works created by students participating in the Guggenheim’s artist-in-residence program, Learning Through Art.

title: Collection in Focus | Piet Mondrian: Ever further
description: Explore a focused exhibition that chronicles the evolution of Piet Mondrian’s signature abstract style.

title: Collection in Focus | The Reach of Faith Ringgold
description: Explore Ringgold’s Woman on a Bridge #1 of 5: Tar Beach, alongside a selection of modern and contemporary works from the museum’s collection.

title: Collection in Focus | Modern European Currents
description: See how artists like Natalia Goncharova, Vasily Kandinsky, and Franz Marc transformed painting in the early 20th century.

title: Collection in Focus | Robert Rauschenberg: Life Can’t Be Stopped
description: Explore the Guggenheim’s Rauschenberg exhibition, featuring Barge (1962–63) and more than a dozen key works marking the 100th anniversary of the artist’s birth.

title: Taryn Simon
description: Experience an interactive installation by Taryn Simon featuring never-before-seen photographs, text, video, and sculpture.

title: Carol Bove
description: From early drawings to monumental compositions in steel, Carol Bove’s first museum survey traces her inventive practice across 25 years.

title: Guggenheim Pop: 1960 to Now
description: From Andy Warhol to Maurizio Cattelan, this exhibition features Pop art from the collection and its enduring influence on contemporary artists.

title: A Year with Children 2026
description: This annual exhibition features works created by students participating in the Guggenheim’s artist-in-residence program, Learning Through Art.

title: Collection in Focus | Zidane, a 21st century portrait
description: Zidane, a 21st century portrait is shown on the occasion of the artwork’s twentieth anniversary and the 2026 FIFA World Cup hosted in North America.

title: Risha Gorig: The Journey
description: The Journey is a sculptural installation by Brooklyn-based artist Risha Gorig that takes the shape of a flock of pink birds soaring in the sky. In this sculpture, one can correlate the migratory nature of birds, the vulnerability and tribulations of embarking on a voyage, with that of human existence. Like many of the artist’s previous works, this piece is kinetic and reminiscent of mobile art.

title: Prospect Park Stroller Walk
description: Calling all parents and caregivers with children! Join a Prospect Park Alliance Naturalist for a leisurely stroll along Prospect Park’s waterways. Embrace the sound of streams and songs of birds, while enjoying fragrant flowers and the towering trees of Brooklyn’s Backyard. Please note that this walk leaves promptly at 10 am from the Prospect Park Boathouse. RSVP to reserve your spot: Please check your email prior to the event for changes, updates and to confirm the walk has met a minimum participant requirement

title: Summer Spruce Up
description: Join Prospect Park Alliance for a special summer work session at our Summer Spruce Up volunteer series. Volunteers will assist in tasks such as invasive plant removal, trail maintenance, litter pick up, and other landscaping needs. The group meets at a different location every week. If you RSVP, you will receive a reminder email with the meeting location. Be advised, these projects involve repetitive bending, kneeling, lifting and transporting loaded wheelbarrows. This opportunity requires using sharp tools and encountering potentially hazardous plants and materials. Appropriate for teens ages 14-17 and adults. Register online or call (929) 888-6826 for more information.

title: Sexual Health Mobile Medical Unit
description: Prospect Park Alliance is collaborating with NewYork-Presbyterian to offer comprehensive sexual health services in Brooklyn’s backyard at Grand Army Plaza every Thursday from 10 am to 3 pm and every Monday at the Parkside + Ocean Avenue Entrance from 10 am to 3 pm. The NewYork-Presbyterian Sexual Health Mobile Medical Unit is a safe, inclusive space for people to receive high-quality sexual health services. Learn more about the Sexual Health Mobile Medical Unit in Prospect Park or reach out to us at mobilehealth@nyp.org or (929) 923-3543. No insurance or ID is required to receive care. Staffed by a team of NewYork-Presbyterian healthcare providers, the mobile medical unit has an exam room and consult room, and offers routine laboratory testing, vaccinations, and PrEP and PEP medications that lower the risk of HIV infection. Patients can also be connected to social services, benefits enrollment, and local healthcare providers. Other services include sexually transmitted infection (STI) testing, family planning, hepatitis C screening, health education and more. The Sexual Health Mobile Medical Unit is made possible through funding from the Dalio Center for Health Justice and the Division of Community and Population Health at NewYork-Presbyterian, and a grant from the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention.

title: Nature Exploration: Pollinator Month
description: Join Prospect Park Alliance for family-friendly nature fun as we explore the question “What is a pollinator?” with park naturalists. Featured Activities: Butterfly Watch, 10 am–3 pm: Learn about butterflies you can find in your neighborhood, their life cycle, and how they help plants thrive in Brooklyn’s Backyard and beyond. Animal Encounter, 1–2 pm: Join Alliance naturalists to learn about the animals in our nature collection. This program starts promptly at 1 pm. Nature on the Go, 2–3 pm: Join an Alliance naturalist to explore the park and learn about the nature around us.

title: Carousel Rides
description: Children’s Corner, $3 per ride, $13 for a book of 5 tickets, Free with Prospect Park Alliance Family Supporter membership or higher. Enjoy a ride on Prospect Park’s beloved 1912 Carousel. Open Thursdays – Sundays and Holidays, this treasured historic attraction features magnificent carved horses, lions, giraffes and deer. Join the Alliance at the Family Supporter level or above and enjoy unlimited free rides!

title: Alfreda's Black Earth Juneteenth Commemoration
description: Join Prospect Park Alliance and Alfreda’s Black Earth for our second annual Juneteenth gathering honoring Black life, land and liberation through film and sound. Our celebration begins with a set by DJ Tara from 4–8 pm, setting the tone for an outdoor celebration grounded in community and reflection. Following, we welcome filmmaker Arielle Knight for a pre-show Q&A. At 8:30 pm, we transition into our film program exploring Black relationships to nature, care and self-determination. Featuring The Boys and the Bees (2025), a tender portrait of a family tending both land and lineage in rural Georgia, and The Learning Tree (1969), Gordon Parks’s landmark coming-of-age film and the first feature directed by a Black American for a major Hollywood studio. Together, these works invite us to consider how Black communities have long cultivated connections—to land, to memory and to one another—as acts of resilience and freedom.

title: Prospect Park Yoga
description: Brooklyn’s most beloved outdoor yoga series is back in Prospect Park! Brooklyn Flow and Prospect Park Alliance present free, outdoor yoga on the Long Meadow. Brooklyn’s finest yoga teachers lead free, outdoor group yoga classes in a low-pressure, beautiful environment. Join hundreds of Brooklynites each week to celebrate yoga and wellness in our treasured Prospect Park. Bring your own mat or towel, a bottle of water and friends. All levels are welcome. Please RSVP and sign the waiver prior to your first class: brooklynflow.com/prospectparkyoga. This outdoor yoga series is sponsored by NewYork-Presbyterian.

title: Uhuru Season: 17 Days of Freedom
description: Visit the Lefferts toll booth to view a series of posters created by local artist Grey Jones. This site-specific installation is on view through Sunday, July 5 and commemorates the historical tradition of celebrating Juneteenth, the last day that Americans were freed from enslavement, and July 5, the day that many Black Americans historically celebrated American Independence as a sociopolitical protest to the continued enslavement of people prior to emancipation.

Nature Exploration, 10 am–3 pm Prospect Park Boathouse, Free Join Prospect Park Alliance for family-friendly nature fun, including seasonal discovery stations and nature-themed activities that will introduce you to the plants, insects and animals that call the park home. title: Ogemdi Ude: MAJOR: Juneteenth Performance
description: Prospect Park Alliance and Danspace Project present MAJOR: Ogemdi Ude‘s dance project exploring the physicality, history, and interiority of majorette dance. These Black femme teams accompanied by marching bands created a movement style that requires master showmanship with allegiance to count, undulation, groove, and sensual yet strong performativity. This celebratory Juneteenth performance is a co-presentation between Danspace Project’s Platform 2026: Secret Gardens and Prospect Park Alliance’s ReImagine Lefferts initiative. This outdoor iteration kicks off MAJOR‘s summer tour of outdoor performances that delve into how technique, effort, and mastery can be attempted, achieved, and abandoned in pursuit of cultural belonging. Ude’s cast of all Black femme dancers and collaborators embraces majorettes as a form and fundamental relic of Black girlhood. Together they pursue the intimate journey of returning to bodies that they thought were lost. A fierce investigation of physical memory, sexuality, sensuality, and community, MAJOR is a nuanced love letter to the folks who taught the team how to be proudly Black and proudly femme. The Chord Archive is showcased alongside performances, a physical and digital documentation of the creative process and personal historical accounts from former majorette dancers. MAJOR had its NYC premiere at New York Live Arts in January 2026.

title: Greenmarket at Grand Army Plaza
description: At what other market can you do all your food shopping, sample local wine and explore Brooklyn’s favorite park? title: Nature Exploration
description: Join Prospect Park Alliance Naturalists to discover why Prospect Park is an important place for over 250 species of birds. Through fun games, experiments and citizen science projects, learn why warblers are so wonderful. Featured Activities: Animal Encounter, 11–12 pm: Join Alliance Naturalists to learn about the animals in our collection. Bird Walk Encounter, 12–1 pm: Did you know 25 types of warblers stop by Prospect Park in Spring? Learn how to identify these cute and colorful birds on a walk around the Boathouse.

title: Park Pitch In: Green and Go
description: Help Prospect Park Alliance keep our park green and vibrant! Explore the park while tackling litter and helping with other greening opportunities. Supplies can be checked out at various locations around the park and include all necessary tools for your volunteer experience. Appropriate for groups, supervised youth ages 9-13, teens and adults. Locations vary by date. Appropriate for groups, supervised youth ages 9-13, teens and adults. Register as an Individual or Register as a Group Questions? Contact the Alliance Volunteer Services team at volunteers@prospectpark.org or 929-888-6826.

title: Pop Up Audubon I
description: Join us at LeFrak Center at Lakeside as we learn about Prospect Park’s watercourse by examining our underwater visitors with magnifying glasses. Featured Activities: Stay + Play, 12–3 pm: Families, children and caregivers can enjoy a range of nature fun including games, puzzles and drawing activities. Pond Water Discovery, 12–3 pm: Did you know that an entire community of living insects, plants and animals makes the Lake their home? Get a closer view with one of our park naturalists. Nature on the Go!, 3–4 pm: Join a park naturalist as we explore the Lake’s shoreline and learn about the wildlife that calls it home. Be a Park Champion by helping to remove trash.

title: Pop-Up Audubon II
description: Join Prospect Park Alliance naturalists to learn about the plants and animals that live in the park’s lake, streams and waterfalls by examining our underwater wildlife with magnifying glasses. Featured Activities: Stay + Play, 12–3 pm: Families, children and caregivers can enjoy a range of nature fun including games, puzzles and drawing activities. Fish Print, 12–3 pm: Create beautiful, nature-inspired art prints using artificial fish in this hands-on activity. Nature on the Go, 3–4 pm: Join a park naturalist as we explore the park’s watercourse and learn about the wildlife that calls it home. Be a Park Champion by helping to remove trash.

title: Lefferts Historic House Open Hours
description: Visit the Lefferts Historic House for family friendly outdoor and indoor exhibits, activities, workshops, and special programs that explore the legacies of resistance and resilience of the Indigenous peoples of Lenapehoking, whose unceded lands the park and house rests upon, and the Africans enslaved by the Lefferts family. View the first ever ReImagine Lefferts Artist in Residence, Adama Delphine Fawundu’s site-specific work, Ancestral Whispers featuring 25 fabric banners that transform the house’s Flatbush Avenue facade, inspired by and honoring the heroism of the 25 Africans enslaved by the Lefferts family, informed by Prospect Park Alliance’s research into the lives of Africans enslaved by the Lefferts family. Learn more about Prospect Park Alliance’s ReImagine Lefferts initiative.

title: Exhibit: The Shirley Chisholm Way: Brooklyn's Blueprint for Change
description: Join Prospect Park Alliance and Shirley Chisholm Cultural Institute (SCCI) for a new exhibit that explores how Brooklyn’s beloved daughter broke down gender and racial barriers to fight for her community, becoming the first Black woman elected to Congress. The exhibit is organized around seven life lessons drawn from Chisholm’s tenacious career. It features stunning portraits of Chisholm by artist Al Johnson and rarely seen photographs and memorabilia from the Congresswoman’s campaigns, curated by Barbara Bullard, Creative Producer and President of SCCI. These materials offer inspirational insights into how Brooklyn’s beloved daughter broke down gender and racial barriers to become the first Black woman to serve in Congress. This exhibit provides visitors with a space to reflect on Chisholm’s legacy as Prospect Park Alliance welcomes the installation of a new monument in her honor at the Parkside and Ocean Avenue entrance in 2027. The exhibit, curated by Barbara Bullard, Cultural Producer and President of the Shirley Chisholm Cultural Institute, is part of Prospect Park Alliance’s ReImagine Lefferts initiative, which is transforming the museum to explore the legacies of resistance and resilience of the Indigenous people of Lenapehoking, whose unceded ancestral lands the park and house rests upon, and Africans who were enslaved by the Lefferts family. The Shirley Chisholm Cultural Institute (SCCI) has been at the forefront of art, culture, and justice and economic sustainability since 1977, with the official permission and participation of Congresswoman Chisholm herself in its inception and development. Its purpose is to preserve the legacy of Congresswoman Shirley Chisholm through the initiatives cultivated during her public service career with 21st century activism and innovation. SCCI is aligned with grassroots groups and frontline communities through creating substantive community-based partnership and collaboration along with strategic interoperative relationships with leaders of governmental, and mainstream organizations.

title: Carousel Rides
description: Children’s Corner, $3 per ride, $13 for a book of 5 tickets, Free with Prospect Park Alliance Family Supporter membership or higher. Enjoy a ride on Prospect Park’s beloved 1912 Carousel. Open Thursdays – Sundays and Holidays, this treasured historic attraction features magnificent carved horses, lions, giraffes and deer. Join the Alliance at the Family Supporter level or above and enjoy unlimited free rides!

title: LGBTQ+ Pride Month: Summer Solstice Pride Stroll
description: Celebrate the summer solstice by setting intentions for the season and learning about traditions to welcome the warm months in nature. Learn more about queer biology of native flora and fauna and learn more about the history of LGBTQ+ New Yorkers in Brooklyn. Note: The walks depart promptly from Grand Army Plaza Arch in Grand Army Plaza.

title: Prospect Park Soiree 2026
description: Get ready for Brooklyn’s most magical gathering—the Prospect Park Soiree! Wear your most festive attire, prepare a delicious feast, bring your favorite bottle of wine and dine and dance under the stars in one of Prospect Park’s most scenic locations. Show off your creativity with your attire and table décor and raise your glass in a toast to Brooklyn’s Backyard. City Winery will be onsite with local wine, beer, cider, and water for sale at the event. Guests may also pre-order wine in advance and have one less item to bring to the park! Event proceeds and a portion of wine sales benefit Prospect Park Alliance, the non-profit organization that sustains, restores and advances Prospect Park for the benefit of the diverse communities that call Brooklyn home. Learn more at prospectpark.org/soiree.

title: Risha Gorig: The Journey
description: The Journey is a sculptural installation by Brooklyn-based artist Risha Gorig that takes the shape of a flock of pink birds soaring in the sky. In this sculpture, one can correlate the migratory nature of birds, the vulnerability and tribulations of embarking on a voyage, with that of human existence. Like many of the artist’s previous works, this piece is kinetic and reminiscent of mobile art.

title: Spoon / Ratboys / Bodega / Alisa Ali
description: Want to see the show from the best seats in the house? Try becoming a SummerStage Member for a Day! Enjoy skip-the-line entry, complimentary beverages, air-conditioned restrooms, and more. Spoon, Austin’s most esteemed rock ambassadors, have released ten albums to date including a string of five straight top 10 records: Ga Ga Ga Ga Ga (2007), Transference (2010), They Want My Soul (2014), Hot Thoughts (2017), and Lucifer on the Sofa (2022). The latter earned the band its first-ever GRAMMY nomination (Best Rock Album) and was praised by Rolling Stone as “the best thing they’ve ever done.” Look for hits from the revered catalog and more when the band play SummerStage in Central Park. They’re joined by Ratboys, a prolific rock band once dubbed “the nicest indie rock band in Chicago” by Pitchfork. They’re touring behind their sixth album Singin’ to an Empty Chair, a record built around a dialogue between Steiner and a close—but estranged—loved one, which swerves between bubbly power-pop and post-country Americana. Opening the show is Brooklyn’s own BODEGA, the anti-consumerist rock band, jagged post-punk energy and social critique, whose new album will be coming out via Chrysalis Records later this Fall. Alisa Ali will DJ for the evening.

title: Duck Down Music presents: Heltah Skeltah & O.G.C. of Boot Camp Clik, Nocturnal & Da Storm 30 Year Anniversary
description: Duck Down Music and the Boot Camp Clik represent an era of New York City hip-hop that focused on the hardworking tenacity of the scene, prioritizing bars over bling. In their late-’90s heyday, the imprint released nine albums including classics from Smif-N-Wessun and Black Moon. The first music to emerge from their label deal with Priority Records would be from newcomers Heltah Skeltah (Rock and the late Sean Price) & O.G.C. (Starang Wondah, Louieville Sluggah, and Top Dog)—first as the supergroup the Fab Five in 1995, and later the following year as separate groups. This show in Bedford-Stuyvesant celebrates the 30th anniversary of those 1996 debuts: Heltah Skeltah’s Nocturnal & O.G.C.’s Da Storm, two albums that explore the city’s edges with sparse beats and cutting wordplay.

title: Shaggy's Yaad: Shaggy with Special Guests Robin Thicke / Tanto Metro & Devonte / Rayvon / Noah Powa / NESTA / Amber Lee / Matthew Malcolm / Shuga in Association with VP Records and Shaggy's Boombastic Radio
description: Want to see the show from the best seats in the house? Try becoming a SummerStage Member for a Day! Enjoy skip-the-line entry, complimentary beverages, air-conditioned restrooms, and more. Fresh off the release of his new album Lottery, global reggae icon Shaggy brings an all-star lineup to Central Park for Shaggy’s Yaad, a free, all-ages celebration of Caribbean music and culture. Presented by VP Records and Shaggy’s Boombastic Radio (SiriusXM Channel 332) as part of our40th anniversary season, the concert will feature performances by Robin Thicke, Tanto Metro & Devonte, Rayvon, Noah Powah, NESTA, Amber Lee, Matthew Malcolm, and Shuga, alongside special guests, DJs Tropical Blendz and Afrique, and hosts DJ Norie, Roxy Romeo and Li’l Nat. Inspired by the Jamaican concept of “yaad” (home), the event brings together music, culture and community for an unforgettable summer night in the heart of New York City.

Nora En Pure Presents PURIFIED / Andhim / Kidnap / Corren Cavini / Jack Emery
description: Want to see the show from the best seats in the house? Try becoming a SummerStage Member for a Day! Enjoy skip-the-line entry, complimentary beverages, air-conditioned restrooms, and more. Since beginning her music career in 2009, Nora en Pure has brought her globally renowned talents to festivals including Coachella, Burning Man, and Tomorrowland. Her breakthrough track, 2013’s “Come With Me,” catapulted her into the spotlight, leading her to produce official remixes for artists such as Faithless, Above & Beyond, Eelke Kleijn and Sofi Tukker. Her production prowess and eye for detail have earned her the nickname “Queen of Deep House.”

title: Mereba / Mack Keane / Arima Ederra / Half Moon
description: In just 10 short years, the German music performance platform COLORSxSTUDIOS has evolved from a simple idea—showcasing emerging artists through stripped-down live performances with a minimalist, monochrome aesthetic—into one of the most influential live-music platforms on the internet. For this show in Bed-Stuy’s Herbert Von King park, they invite to the heart of Brooklyn a trio of cutting edge artists on the cusp of breaking out. Mereba, a talented singer and songwriter with a deep connection to the American South who effortlessly flits between hip-hop, folk, and progressive R&B, has already recorded with both big indies (Secretly Canadian), major labels (Interscope), and Dreamville’s Spillage Village collective. Her debut 2019 LP The Jungle Is The Only Way Out was co-produced by Samuel Hoffman and 9th Wonder. She’s joined by Mack Keane, the Los Angeles native emerging as a leader of the next wave of R&B. His self-produced 2025 project Entries demonstrated his command of songwriting and storytelling, earning him a spot opening for Destin Conrad on the Love On Digital Tour. He has since partnered with Star Trak Entertainment – the label behind some of the most defining sounds in R&B – and is gearing up to release his debut album Wide Eyed on May 8. Opening the show is Arima Ederra, who imbued melancholy with bursts of sunshine on her debut LP An Orange Colored Day; her latest album A Rush to Nowhere takes a morose ‘80s rock sentimentality to bolder and darker places. With DJ sets from Half Moon, the hip internet radio station turned music media platform and “new age cultural institution” with its finger on the pulse of Black dance music in New York City.

title: MIKE's Young World: Max B / Thirteendegrees° / ERISTHEPLANET / SALIMATA / Niontay / Black Noi$e / MIKE with The Band of the Century
description: As rapper and curator MIKE’s career has grown from teenage phenom into budding label owner, so has the size of his tent. His annual Young World festival emerged from the intimate Abrons Art Center in lower Manhattan into the sprawling block party it’s become today, taking over a sizable portion of Bed-Stuy’s largest park. This year MIKE curates a line up headlined by Max B, the legendary Harlem MC who rose to prominence in the mid 2000s. Despite a 16 year incarceration from 2009-2025, he maintained a strong influence on hip-hop’s sound and a cult-like fanbase that’s global, but most pronounced in his hometown of NYC. For the first time in front of a wide audience, MIKE will be backed by “the Band of the Century” – the band he developed for his NPR Tiny Desk concert. Cutting-edge genre pioneers Thirteendegrees°, ERISTHEPLANET, SALIMATA, Niontay, and Black Noi$e will also perform.

title: Ruidosa Fest: Pabllo Vittar presents CLUB VITTAR
Bilal / GENA / DJ Tara
description: Presented with the legendary Apollo Theater, this evening brings together boundary-pushing voices in R&B and soul. A foundational member of the loose late-’90s hip-hop/R&B collective the Soulquarians, Bilal has an effortless, understated swag. It oozes from every track of his 2001 debut 1st Born Second, a retro yet fresh take on soul music at the turn of the century. He’s gotten more experimental as he’s grown, collaborating with artists such as Kimbra, Otis Brown III, Kat Dahlia, Slum Village, and Kendrick Lamar on his third album To Pimp a Butterfly. Bilal’s latest release is Live at Glasshaus, a live recording from an intimate, career-spanning performance with Questlove, Robert Glasper, and Burniss Travis as his backing band. He’s joined by GENA, a new project from the dynamic R&B artist Liv.e and renowned producer/drummer Karriem Riggins; their name stands for God Energy, Naturally Amazing, indicative of their improvisationally ordained approach to making music. On the ones and twos is DJ Tara, host of misc. on The Lot Radio, and co-founder of Donuts are Forever, the longest running J Dilla tribute and fundraiser in the world.

title: Bastille Day: Black M / Laurent Voulzy / Legends of the 80’s / Michel Polnareff
description: For Bastille Day, the Consulate General of France in New York imports four distinct acts from across the French pop landscape. Headlining is Black M, French-Guinean rapper and member of the world-renowned group Sexion d’Assaut. Known for his animated personality and tongue-twisting rhyming style, he broke out as a solo artist with the 2014 album Les yeux plus gros que le monde, and has collaborated with artists such as Shakira (“Comme Moi”), Manu Dibango, and Amadou & Mariam. He’s joined by Laurent Voulzy, one of the most successful and critically acclaimed composers in contemporary French pop who has only become more prolific with age. Often working in tandem with longtime collaborator Alain Souchon, Voulzy is known for an obsessive attention to detail in the studio, crafting sophisticated melodies and rich arrangements. Also featured, Legends of the 80’s, a group of some of the most legendary French artists of the ‘80s. They’ve been touring as a unit for more than two decades, sharing nostalgia and a love of one of France’s most popular eras in music. Finally, Michel Polnareff, the enigmatic French singer and composer best known for his 1966 hits “La Poupée qui fait non” and “Love Me, Please Love Me,” who has found success writing pop hits, film scores, and music for the stage.

title: Morocco: From Dakhla to Casablanca: Hoba Hoba Spirit / Hamid El Kasri / Dakhla Casa Xpress / Mr ID / NDRK
description: In association with the Moroccan Ministry of Youth, Culture, and Communication, the Council of the Moroccan Community Abroad, and Maroc Festivals, SummerStage brings a handful of artists showcasing the diversity of sounds and rhythms from the North African nation to Central Park. Headlining is Hoba Hoba Spirit, a Moroccan rock band from Casablanca that fuses rock, reggae and Gnawa (a Sufi style brought from West Africa) into a sound they call “haiha”—evoking the controlled chaos and randomness of their live experience. They joined by Hamid El Kasri, a Gnawa master trained in the Chemeli style with a distinctively deep and rich voice who performs with the three-stringed bass guembri; Dakhla Casa Xpress beginning with the stillness and warmth of the desert, and building toward the electric energy of the city; Mr ID, the DJ and producer who brought American rap beats, funk rhythms and the spirit of Jazz back home with him to Morocco, where he fused the sounds with violin airs and African percussion; and NDRK, a rising star on the dance floors of Marrakech, Casablanca, and Dubai, who also produces gems like the track selected as the anthem for the Paris 2024 Olympic Games

title: Mavis Staples / Brother Wallace / LaGuardia High School Gospel Ensemble / Luv
description: Mavis Staples has been performing for eight decades. At 86 years old, she’s known as one of the most enduring figures in American music, earning inductions into multiple halls of fame (blues, rock, and gospel), was a Kennedy Center Honoree, and winner of numerous GRAMMY awards, including one for lifetime achievement. With her return to SummerStage, she’s sure to perform the hits alongside new tracks from her 15th solo album Sad and Beautiful World. She’s collaborated with nearly every major figure of her many eras, from Bob Dylan to Prince, Aretha Franklin, and Willie Nelson. Opening the evening is Brother Wallace, a force of modern soul and gospel sound. Most recently, he’s collaborated with The Heavy guitarist Dan Taylor to build a body of work built on his electrifying brand of soul music. The LaGuardia Gospel Ensemble explores the rich history and enduring legacy of gospel music, tracing its evolution from the early 20th century to today while honoring its roots in the Black Church and the struggle for social change. Through powerful performances, the ensemble celebrates one of America’s most influential musical traditions while fostering artistry, community, and the next generation of performers.

title: De La Soul / Pete Rock / DJ Hannibal Buress
description: At the dawn of the ‘90s, when the most popular hip-hop was neck-deep in intimidating street tales, New Yorkers De La Soul offered an alternate take, striving for peace and harmony with creative rhyme styles on their critically acclaimed debut 3 Feet High and Rising. Prince Paul’s heavily sample-based production style and inventive skits remain massively influential, even as hip-hop aesthetics have shifted from the jazz, soul, and classic R&B sounds he drew from. The group continued to evolve, leaving behind the flowery aesthetic that mistakenly branded them as “hippies” for a darker tone on records like De La Soul is Dead and Buhloone Mindstate. Though this current lineup is without Trugoy the Dove—who passed in 2023—the group has experienced a resurgence of interest with new 2025 release Cabin in the Sky. They’re joined by Bronx legend Pete Rock, producer and occasional MC best known for his collaborations with CL Smooth (“They Reminisce Over You (T.R.O.Y.)”) and his production work on classic albums by Nas, AZ, Rakim, and his cousin Heavy D. Widely considered one of the most renowned hip-hop producers of the ‘90s, his hazy, jazz-centric sample style is both distinctive and widely influential. His recent studio collaboration with Common, The Auditorium Vol. 1, earned him a GRAMMY nomination last year. Actor, comedian, and musician Hannibal Buress will open the evening with a DJ set.

title: Anjunadeep: Open Air New York City: Because of Art / Durante / Ezequiel Arias / James Grant & Jody Wisternoff / Marsh / Romain Garcia / SunrYse
description: SummerStage Member Presale: Tuesday, February 17 at 12PM Capital One Cardholder Presale: Tuesday, February 17 at 12PM General on-sale: Wednesday, February 18 at 12PM Founded in 2005, Anjunadeep is the supergroup Above & Beyond’s label, dedicated to highlighting deeper, more progressive sounds in the club and electronic music scene. With a catalogue spanning nearly 15 years, the label has brought artists such as Lane 8, Ben Böhmer, Dusky, and Eli & Fur to the world stage. Their shows have drawn audiences from Brooklyn to London, selling out across 15 countries and five continents with their friendly, welcoming, and always bumping party vibe. With releases that range from melodic house, electronic, ambient, to techno, the diversity and integrity of the label’s sound has earned Anjunadeep a fiercely loyal following around the globe and a reputation as one of dance music’s most respected imprints. For their show in Central Park, they’re sure to bring some of their best beats.

title: DJ Rekha's Basement Bhangra Beyond: Jaz Dhami / Mitika Kanwar / Babbulicious / Rajuju / DJ Rekha + special guests
description: For the fourth consecutive year, cultural instigator DJ Rekha takes the energy of their legendary Basement Bhangra party beyond the club walls with a multi-genre live show, infusing sounds of hip-hop, dancehall, and diasporic grooves to the World’s Fair grounds at Queens’ Flushing Meadows-Corona Park. This year’s lineup is anchored by British-Indian singer Jaz Dhami, known for redefining the face of Punjabi music through hits such as “High Heels,” “Zulfa,” “Beparwaiyan,” and his latest single “Saviour.” Also featured is Mitika Kanwar, whose unique blend of Punjabi lofi folk and R&B includes collaborations with Sidhu Moosewala, Nucleya, and Talwiinder. Babbulicious, a Punjabi-Canadian singer and multidisciplinary artist, brings a joyful energy with crowd favorites like “Gaddi Red Challenger,” recently performed at New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani’s inauguration. The lineup is rounded out by DJ Rekha, who, alongside master Bhangra dancer Kala Sher and special guests, delivers a ground-shaking, full-body experience reflecting the eclectic vibe of the World’s Borough.

title: Complexions Contemporary Ballet / Paul Taylor Dance Company / LaTasha Barnes’ The Jazz Continuum presents The Look of Love
description: The Joyce Theater brings three contemporary dance companies to Central Park, celebrating Jazz music through a wide range of styles. SummerStage alum Complexions Contemporary Ballet, founded in 1994 by Dwight Rhoden and Desmond Richardson, will perform Rhoden’s latest company premiere Midnight Riff, which celebrates legendary female vocalists including Billie Holiday and Sarah Vaughn, set to recordings by New York Voices. Led by Artistic Director Michael Novak, The Paul Taylor Dance Company, founded in 1954, makes its festival debut with the vibrant work by Resident Choreographer, Robert Battle, Under The Rhythm, set to music by Ella Fitzgerald, Wycliffe Gordon, and more. Finally, multi-Bessie award winner (2021/2023), New York Times-lauded “Queen of Social Dance” and celebrated Best Dance & Breakout Star (2021) LaTasha Barnes is an internationally awarded and critically-acclaimed artist-scholar, curator, choreographer, and tradition-bearer of Black American Social Dance, co-based in Phoenix, AZ, and New York. Barnes presents an excerpt from her full-length solo “The Look of Love” that reflects on how love has shaped her life. Performed with a live band, this piece blends recorded voices from her chosen “Framily” into the soundscape, which Barnes experiences as a deeply embodied, physical sensation. Through hip-hop, swing, house, and funk, she creates a heartfelt love letter to her influences—and a promise to those who came before her to love boldly and without fear. One of the only theaters built by dancers for dance, The Joyce Theater has garnered a well-deserved international reputation for cultivating and honoring emerging and established dance artists whose work represents a wide range of aesthetic interests. Since opening in 1982, The Joyce has supported the dance community by providing a home for more than 400 domestic and international companies and by offering an annual 45 to 48-week season which, each year, allows more than 150,000 audience members to experience diverse, popular and challenging performances. The Joyce’s invaluable commitment to artists and audiences extends beyond the immense volume of work taking place on its stage. In an effort to make dance accessible to audiences throughout New York City, The Joyce has also expanded its reach beyond its Chelsea home through off-site presentations at venues ranging in scope from Lincoln Center’s David H. Koch Theater, to Brooklyn’s Invisible Dog Art Center, and to outdoor programming in such spaces as Hudson River Park. The Joyce anticipates many more years of success in sustaining dance by building robust, educated audiences who share a love of the art form and embrace its enduring power.

description: SummerStage has teamed up with Festival Latinidades and Afro-Latino Festival NYC to spotlight some of the world’s most talented Afro-Latina musicians. The lineup features Luedji Luna, one of the leading voices in contemporary Brazilian music, celebrated for her powerful artistry and commitment to amplifying Black narratives, alongside a very special guest: the outstanding Liniker. The two Latin GRAMMY-winning singer-songwriters collaborated on “Harém,” a soulful track that has garnered hundreds of thousands of plays. Also appearing is Susana Baca, the Peruvian singer-songwriter, educator, folklorist, ethnomusicologist, and three-time Latin GRAMMY winner who once served as Peru’s Minister of Culture. Rounding out the bill is Afro-Latino Festival founder and singer-songwriter Mai-Elka Prado, performing with the Brooklyn-based Lush Song Collective. title: Julieta Venegas / Lasso / Micro TDH / DJ Anamaria Sayre
description: A sampling of Spanish-language pop hits Central Park, in association with the Latin Alternative Music Conference. GRAMMY-winning Mexican singer-songwriter Julieta Venegas has been a mainstay of Mexican pop since her breakout 2003 LP Sí, a lovestruck collection of sun-drenched hits that’s been described as “relentlessly perky.” She’s best known for the catchy pop found on that record and its follow-up Limon y Sal, but the one-time member of ska band Tijuana No! debuted with the significantly edgier rock en español records Aqui and Bueninvento, and has written songs for and performed with a wide range of Latin artists, from Paulina Rubio and Mala Rodríguez to Miguel Bosé and Los Tigres del Norte. She’s joined by the Venezuelan singer and composer Lasso, known for his brand of Latin pop, rock, and romantic ballads; his 2011 debut album Sin Otro Sentido made him an overnight star in Venezuela (and landed him a starring role in a TV soap opera). He would later win the Best Pop Rock Latin GRAMMY for “Ojos Marrones” in 2023.

title: La India & MD Bobby Allende / DJ Tony Touch / DJ Lucho
description: Linda Viera Caballero quickly made a name for herself as La India, one of the biggest voices in salsa. But before Celia Cruz would dub her the “Princess of Salsa,” she got her start in the South Bronx, where she was a founding member of the Latin Freestyle group TKA, and cut a solo record with Freestyle legends John “Jellybean” Benitez and her husband “Little” Louie Vega. After “Llegó La India via Eddie Palmieri,” she blossomed into salsa royalty and never looked back, going on to work with Cruz and Tito Puente, and winning Best Salsa Album Latin GRAMMY for her album Intensamente India Con Canciones De Juan Gabriel. She’s joined by DJ Tony Touch of The Originals on the ones and twos.

title: UBB 40th Anniversary Celebration: BreakBeat Lou & Lord Finesse / Diamond D / Grand Puba / Sadat X / Nice & Smooth / Peter Gunz / DJ Boogie Blind
description: Few records—if any—have had as much impact on music as the Ultimate Breaks and Beats compilation assembled by BreakBeat Lou and BreakBeat Lenny in 1986. By collecting what they called the “foundational” breakbeats – the very records DJs looped for breakdancers at the earliest hip-hop park jams and block parties, they created not only an essential tool for DJs, but also an accidental archive of hip-hop’s DNA. For this 40th anniversary celebration of the original compilation—of which only 25 volumes were released between 1986 and 1991— Breakbeat Lou is joined by members of the Bronx’s legendary Diggin’ in the Crates crew, Lord Finesse and Diamond D. As pioneers of a distinct New York sound, their work helped define the golden era of hip-hop production and lyricism that would soon resonate nationwide. Rounding out a line up filled with SummerStage alumni are Grand Puba and Sadat X of the acclaimed socially conscious rap group Brand Nubian; the iconic duo Nice & Smooth, best known for their single “Hip-Hop Junkies,” their feature on Big Daddy Kane’s “Pimpin Ain’t Easy,” and their unforgettable appearance on Gang Starr’s “DWYCK”; and Peter Gunz, whose collaboration with Lord Tariq, “Deja Vu (Uptown Baby)” became a late-90s anthem—sampling the unmistakable groove of Steely Dan. Completing the celebration is a set by DJ Boogie Blind, known for his affiliations with the legendary X‑ecutioners as well as the Diggin’ in the Crates collective. Together, these artists represent the culture, creativity, and crate-digging spirit that helped transform a DJ tool into one of hip-hop’s most influential musical documents.

title: Doug E. Fresh & Funk Flex Second Annual Hip-Hop Appreciation Park Jam
description: “Human beatbox” Doug E. Fresh is one of the pivotal figures of hip-hop, who can accurately imitate drum machines and effects with just his mouth and a microphone. His early collaborations with Slick Rick — ”The Show” and “La Di Da Di” — are among the genre’s foundational hits. Funk Flex is more than just a DJ. He’s a hip-hop institution, a radio and club mainstay who has helped some of New York’s biggest artists and hits break out and continues to keep his finger on the pulse of hip-hop and R&B. These SummerStage alums are back with an old-school park jam reminiscent of hip-hop’s humble and joyous beginnings, celebrating the art form in which they’ve invested their lives, in the borough in which it was born.

title: GHANAFEST-NY: Edem / Andy Dosty
description: The annual tradition of GhanaFest-NY once again brings its celebration of Ghanaian culture and heritage to the residents of New York City. This year’s event features music from popular Ghanaian rapper Edem, who debuted in 2009 with The Volta Regime and won the Best International Act-Africa at the 2015 Black Canadian Awards. The video for his 2016 song “Nyedzilo” was nominated for three WatsUp TV Africa Music Video Awards. On the ones and twos is Andy Dosty, the Ghanaian DJ and radio presenter with Ghana’s Multimedia Group Limited; he’s hosted Daybreak Hitz on Hitz FM since 2017.

title: Trueno / Milo J / J Noa
description: A trio of rising Latin hip-hop stars head to Central Park for this show, presented in association with the Latin Alternative Music Conference. Of the various figures in the red hot Argentinian rap scene, none has shined brighter than Trueno, the son of Uruguayan rapper Peligro and a veteran of the scene’s battle rap circuit. His early appearance on producer Bizarrap’s freestyle show BZRP Freestyle Sessions launched him to nationwide fame and helped establish the show as a chart-topping vehicle, landing the pair at the number one spot on Argentina’s Hot 100. He’s since released several solo albums, collaborated with the likes of Bad Gyal, Feid, and J Balvin, and is responsible for one of the Argentinian rap scene’s biggest hits in 2020’s “Mamichula.” He’s joined by Milo J, who leveraged his viral hit “Milagrosa” into collaborations with Nicki Nicole, Duki, and Peso Pluma; he also benefited from an appearance on Bizarrap’s YouTube series, which led to the pair’s collaborative EP En Dormir Sin Madrid. His latest LP La Vida Era Más Corta saw him take a hard left turn into art-pop, fusing Argentine folklore with urban and experimental sounds. Opening the show is the dynamic Dominican rapper J Noa, a nimble-tongued MC with a rapid-fire flow who stunned the world at her NPR Tiny Desk performance, her first outside of her own neighborhood in the Dominican Republic.

WayV / THE EITHER / Chopstix
description: For a century, China Institute of America has been dedicated to deepening the world’s understanding of China. In celebration of their Centennial, SummerStage brings two takes on the future sounds of Chinese pop to Central Park. The K-pop boy band NCT is so massive that it’s become its own ecosystem, spinning off members into their own sub-groups that establish their own voice. WayV are the fourth such to spin off—and its first China-based collective—recording pop songs that fuse hip-hop and dance with R&B-stylized vocals sung in Mandarin since their 2019 debut EP The Vision. They’re joined by THE EITHER, formed in New York in 2017. Their sound — Post-Orientalism — rebuilds Eastern elements for today, blending electrified traditional instruments, Chinese opera-inspired vocals, progressive rock energy, and compositions with a strong Eastern melodic identity into a bold, future-facing performance.

title: Philharmonia Baroque: Of Rivers and Roses
Andrew Bird with Wordless Music Orchestra 20th Anniversary Performance of "The Mysterious Production of Eggs" / Surma
description: The violinist, singer, songwriter, and composer Andrew Bird has stretched the musical boundaries of folk in an eclectic career that spans 18 albums, developing a multi-layered sound with diverse instrumentation. His most defining work is arguably 2005’s The Mysterious Production of Eggs, which demonstrated an avant-pop sophistication with a mastery of elaborate loops, multi-tracked violin, and his remarkable prowess as a whistler. Bird has headlined shows at iconic venues such as Carnegie Hall, the Sydney Opera House, Walt Disney Concert Hall, and Red Rocks Amphitheatre, but he’ll celebrate the 20th anniversary of that record at SummerStage in Central Park with the Wordless Music Orchestra, performing the record for the first time in its entirety alongside a full orchestra, followed by a suite of fan-favorites from his nearly 30-year repertoire. Surma’s presentation is presented with support from the Arte Institute. Learn more about Arte Institute at https://arteinstitute.org/.

title: Funk Flex Birthday R&B Picnic: El DeBarge / Jon B / Fonda Rae / Taana Gardner / Strafe / Dres From Blacksheep / DJ M U / Music by Funk Flex
description: Funk Flex has been spinning hip-hop and R&B across NYC’s airwaves for the better part of four decades, becoming a hip-hop institution. His R&B picnic in Central Park celebrates his birthday and this year he is bringing some R&B heavy-hitters for a not-to-be-missed throwback show. They include Jon B, a platinum-selling R&B artist discovered by Babyface whose first three LPs climbed the Billboard 200 album chart and whose collaboration with 2Pac, ”Are U Still Down,” was among the late rapper’s final recordings; Fonda Rae, an American R&B singer best known for her club hits like “Over Like a Fat Rat” and “Touch Me”; Taana Gardner, who saw early success in disco with West End Records in the late ‘70s-early ‘80s, and whose 1981 single “Heartbeat” was famously remixed by Larry Levan into a staple at the Paradise Garage; Strafe, aka Brooklyn native and producer Steve Standard, who is best known for the 1984 single “Set It Off,” which has been extensively sampled in hip-hop, house, and techno circles; and Dres, best known as one half of the duo Black Sheep, the Native Tongues group whose debut LP A Wolf in Sheep’s Clothing remains a foundation of New York City’s early ‘90s boom-bap era. Joining Flex on the ones and twos spinning records between sets is his fellow Hot97 DJ M U.

title: Funk Flex Birthday Fest: Crystal Waters / Cynthia / Frankie Cutlass / Force MDs / Sunshine Anderson / Greg Nice/ CL Smooth
description: When you’re a personality as big as Funk Flex, you celebrate your birthday all weekend. This party, on the second day of the New York hip-hop institution’s birthday weekend, features another collection of legacy acts from Funk Flex’s considerable sphere of influence. The headliner is Crystal Waters, the house and dance music singer known for hits like “100% Pure Love” and house staple “Gypsy Woman (She’s Homeless).” She’s joined by Cynthia, the Puerto Rican freestyle singer who had a hit with her Johnny O collaboration “Dreamboy/Dreamgirl” and her cover of the Stylistics’ “Break Up to Make Up”; Frankie Cutlass, a member of Funk Flex’s DJ collective The Flip Squad whose hook from his classic single “Puerto Rico” became a staple of Funk Flex’s sets; Force MDs, Staten Island’s own R&B group that fused doo wop and hip-hop and peaked with their Top 10 pop hit “Tender Love” in 1985; Sunshine Anderson, the North Carolina R&B and soul singer who had a hit in 2001 with her debut single “Heard It All Before”; Mr Cheeks, formerly of the Lost Boyz, the Queens trio responsible for “Lifestyles of the Rich & Shameless” and “Renee,” who went solo in 1999 and had a hit with his Missy Elliott and Petey Pablo collaboration “Lights, Camera, Action!”; and CL Smooth, one half of the duo with Pete Rock responsible for foundational works such as “They Reminisce Over You (T.O.N.Y.).”

title: Songbook Sundays: The Tony Bennett Centennial
Bobby Sanabria Multiverse Big Band / Charlie Aponte / Judy Santos / DJ Connie & DJ Medina
description: A graduate of the Berklee College of Music – drummer, percussionist, composer, arranger, documentary film producer, activist, radio host, writer, and multi-Grammy nominated as a leader, Bobby Sanabria is a native Nuyorican son of the South Bronx. His versatility as both a drummer and percussionist from small group to big band has become legendary. He’s performed and recorded with every major figure in the history of Latin jazz, jazz, and salsa, with such legends as Dizzy Gillespie, Tito Puente, Mongo Santamaria, Eddie Palmieri, Paquito D’Rivera, Candido, Charles McPherson, Larry Harlow, Ruben Blades, Celia Cruz, and the Father of Afro-Cuban jazz, Mario Bauzá where he was his drummer for 9 years touring and recording on his major GRAMMY nominated albums. For more than 40 years, Charlie Aponte was the lead vocalist for the legendary El Gran Combo de Puerto Rico, one of the island’s most successful salsa groups of all time and a salsa institution. One of his earliest performances with the band included a 1973 show in front of 50,000 fans at Yankee Stadium as the opening act for the Fania All-Stars’ sold-out concert. With El Gran Combo, Aponte performed all over the world, sold millions of records, and won multiple Latin GRAMMYs. Aponte left the group in 2014 and quickly released his debut solo album Una Nueva Historia the next year. His 2017 album Pa’ Mi Gente was nominated for the Latin GRAMMY for Best Salsa Album. He’s joined by Judy Santos, an American singer best known for her work with the Dominican-American bachata group Aventura. She collaborated on three of their albums in the early aughts, including the song “Obsesión,””Angelito,” and “La Guerra,” her 2002 duet with Romeo Santos (no relation) that topped the charts in several Latin American countries.
